The Soul of Central
Lombok

items.title:
MotoGP Pertamina
Grand Prix of Indonesia

paragraphs: Centuries ago, a princess named Mandalika chose the sea over a throne. Her sacrifice became a ritual, her name became a legacy — and her spirit still lives in every wave that breaks along Central Lombok's shores.

For generations, the Sasak people carried that spirit through their weaving, their music, and their dances. A culture built not on conquest, but on devotion. Today, the same shores where Bau Nyale is celebrated every year now echo with the roar of MotoGP engines. Central Lombok hasn't changed — it has simply shown the world who it always was.

Discover.title: Discover Central Lombok - PrayaCity
Discover.intro: From ancient legends to world-class events — Central Lombok is more than a destination, it's an experience waiting to be lived.

items.title: History
items.description: From the mighty Majapahit expeditions to the rise of Sasak kingdoms, Central Lombok carries centuries of history within its soil. Discover how a land shaped by legend became a cornerstone of Indonesian heritage.

items.title: Culture
items.description: Witness sacred rituals passed down for generations, hear the thunder of Gendang Beleq drums, and taste flavors so bold they redefine your idea of Indonesian cuisine. Central Lombok's culture is lived every single day.

items.title: Destinations
items.description: From white-sand beaches with pepper-shaped grains found nowhere else on earth, to rolling green hills overlooking the Indian Ocean — Central Lombok holds landscapes that defy expectation. Your perfect destination is already here.

items.title: Programs & Events
items.description: Home to the first street circuit in the world to host MotoGP, Central Lombok is where tradition and ambition collide. Explore a packed calendar of events that draw visitors from across the globe.

Cta.description1: Whether you're chasing perfect waves, ancient traditions — Central Lombok has a story written just for you.
Cta.description2: Explore at your own pace, or let our AI guide lead you to exactly what you're looking for.

Footer.tagline: Your digital gateway to Central Lombok.

CultureHero.title: Ancient Traditions and Bold Flavors
CultureHero.description: Central Lombok's culture is not something you observe from a distance — it is something you feel, taste, and carry with you long after you leave. From the sacred Bau Nyale ritual that honors a princess who gave her life for peace, to the thundering rhythm of Gendang Beleq drums echoing through village ceremonies, every tradition here has survived centuries not by coincidence, but by devotion.

CultureTraditions.subtitle: Traditions & Rituals
CultureTraditions.titleRest: That Have Stood the Test of Time

tradition1.title: Bau Nyale — When the Sea Comes Alive
tradition1.description: Every year at Seger Beach, thousands gather to catch nyale sea worms — believed to be the reincarnation of Princess Mandalika, who sacrificed herself to bring peace among her suitors. As dawn breaks, the people of Central Lombok wade into the waters in a moment of spiritual connection that has been repeated for centuries.

tradition2.title: Peresean — Where Courage is Tested
tradition2.description: Two men, rattan sticks, and buffalo hide shields — Peresean is a traditional Sasak duel that symbolizes bravery and sportsmanship. Once held to invoke rain during dry seasons, it is today celebrated at cultural festivals across Central Lombok, drawing crowds who come to witness a living expression of Sasak identity.

tradition3.title: Gendang Beleq — The Pulse of the Island
tradition3.description: Translating to "Big Drum," Gendang Beleq is an ancient musical tradition originally used to encourage warriors marching to battle. Today, these massive, thunderous drums form the heartbeat of traditional Sasak wedding processions and cultural ceremonies, echoing the island's fierce and vibrant history.

tradition4.title: Sade Village Weaving — Threads of Time
tradition4.description: In the traditional Sasak village of Sade, the meticulous art of hand-weaving has been passed down through generations of women. Using natural dyes and intricate backstrap looms, they create stunning Songket fabrics that tell stories of their ancestors, where every pattern holds a profound cultural significance.

tradition5.title: Alang-Alang — The Sacred Granary
tradition5.description: Across Central Lombok, you'll spot the iconic lumbung (rice barns) with their distinctive curved thatched roofs. More than just agricultural storage, these structures represent the community's profound gratitude for the harvest and are central to communal gatherings and thanksgiving rituals.

tradition6.title: Waktu Telu — The Ancient Syncretism
tradition6.description: Waktu Telu represents a unique ancestral belief system that blends Islamic teachings with indigenous animist and Hindu-Buddhist traditions. Though practiced by fewer today, its philosophy of harmony between humans, nature, and the divine remains deeply woven into the spiritual fabric of Central Lombok.

HistoryPage.heroTitle: Centuries of History, One Remarkable Land
HistoryPage.timelineDescription: Central Lombok's history spans more than seven centuries — from the arrival of Majapahit forces to the roar of MotoGP engines on the Mandalika Circuit.

timelineItems.title: The Majapahit Expedition
timelineItems.description: Central Lombok first entered recorded history when the Majapahit Empire sent an expedition led by Mpu Nala to the island of Lombok. This marked the beginning of Hindu-Javanese cultural influence that would shape the region's early civilization and lay the groundwork for the kingdoms that followed.

timelineItems.title: The Rise of Sasak Kingdoms
timelineItems.description: Following the Majapahit expedition, two powerful kingdoms emerged in Central Lombok — the Kingdom of Pejanggik and the Kingdom of Langko. These kingdoms established sophisticated governance systems and became the political and cultural centers of the Sasak people, whose identity and traditions remain deeply rooted in Central Lombok to this day.

timelineItems.title: The Arrival of Islam
timelineItems.description: In the 16th century, Islam arrived in Lombok through Sunan Prapen, son of Sunan Giri — one of the nine Islamic saints of Java. Entering through the Kingdom of Selaparang, his teachings spread peacefully across the island, blending with existing Sasak traditions to form a unique Islamic culture that defines Central Lombok's spiritual identity to this day.

timelineItems.title: Foreign Influence & Resistance
timelineItems.description: Like much of Indonesia, Central Lombok did not escape the reach of colonial powers. The region endured periods of foreign control that tested the resilience of its people. Yet through every challenge, the Sasak community preserved their language, their customs, and their sense of identity — a testament to the strength of a culture deeply rooted in its own land.

timelineItems.title: From Heritage to the World Stage
timelineItems.description: Today, Central Lombok stands at the intersection of tradition and ambition. The establishment of KEK Mandalika, the construction of the Pertamina Mandalika International Circuit, and the arrival of MotoGP have transformed this historically rich region into a globally recognized destination — without ever losing sight of the legends and traditions that made it extraordinary in the first place.
